description: Adversarial forged closure chain: a Class D conduct artifact whose stage 2 hash is byte-identical to stage 1's hash (a forged collision under different content) MUST be rejected as CONDUCT_INVALID. Distinct stage content MUST yield distinct stage hashes; identical hashes under non-identical preimages are either a hash collision or a forgery and MUST NOT be accepted.

input.claim: this strategy is recommended on the basis of a forged commit chain

why: WiseOrder Protocol v0.1.0 invariants CC1, CC2: a hash without its preimage is unauditable, and later stages MUST declare dependency on prior stage hashes. Stage 1 and stage 2 declare distinct content (values_frame vs alternatives_summary) but identical hashes. Under SHA-256 over distinct canonicalized content this is structurally impossible without (a) a SHA-256 collision (vanishingly improbable) or (b) a forgery (the more likely explanation). A conformant verifier MUST treat an identical-hash-different-content claim as a structural defect and emit CONDUCT_INVALID. Mitigates threat D-1 (post-hoc rationalization) by closing the loophole of declaring a 'committed' stage chain whose internal hashes do not actually witness the declared content.
